title: Favicon Generator
shortDescription: Generate favicon sizes from one image file directly in your browser with instant downloads.
metaTitle: Favicon Generator - Create Favicon Sizes Online
metaDescription: Create favicon sizes from one image in your browser. Generate multiple favicon files instantly with fast downloads and no upload required.
intro: Create favicons from a single image file in your browser. Quickly generate the common icon sizes you need for websites and download them instantly.
overview: This favicon generator helps you prepare website icons from one source image without installing software. It runs locally in your browser, so your image stays on your device while you generate the sizes you need for tabs, bookmarks, and app icons.
howItWorks: Upload or paste an image file as the source.
The tool creates a set of favicon sizes automatically.
Preview the generated results in your browser.
useCases: Create a favicon for a new website.
Generate icon sizes for browser tabs and bookmarks.
Prepare app and PWA icon assets quickly.
Resize a logo into multiple favicon formats.

examples.title: Single logo to favicon set
examples.output: Get multiple favicon sizes ready to download.
examples.description: Best for turning a brand mark into consistent website icons.

examples.title: Fast client-side generation
examples.input: Choose an image from your device.
examples.output: Generate icons without sending files to a server.
examples.description: Useful when you want a quick, private workflow.

faq.question: What image types can I use?
faq.answer: Use a standard image file such as PNG, JPG, or SVG if supported by your browser and the tool interface.

faq.question: Are my files uploaded to a server?
faq.answer: No. The favicon generation runs in your browser, so the file is processed locally on your device.

faq.question: How many favicon sizes are generated?
faq.answer: The tool creates the common favicon sizes needed for modern websites, depending on the source image and output settings.

faq.question: Can I use a non-square image?
faq.answer: Yes, but a square source image usually gives the cleanest favicon result.
